candidates.title: The Sensitivity Floor: Why Absorbed and Non-Absorbed Features Are Both Insensitive

collisions.paper: Tian et al., 2025. Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity. arXiv:2509.23717
collisions.overlap: Documents sensitivity decline with SAE width; paraphrase AUC protocol. Does NOT connect sensitivity to absorption or geometric structure

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Defines absorption detection protocol via first-letter classification. Does NOT study joint distribution with sensitivity or propose complementary failure mechanisms

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2026. Sanity Checks for Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2602.14111
collisions.overlap: Shows random baselines match SAEs on interpretability/causal editing. Does NOT mechanistically explain the Sanity Check through sensitivity floor

collisions.paper: Bereska et al., 2025. Superposition as Lossy Compression. arXiv:2512.13568
collisions.overlap: Information-theoretic framework for superposition/compression. Does NOT propose sensitivity floor or connect to specificity/reliability tradeoff

collisions.paper: Michaud et al., 2025. Understanding SAE Scaling with Feature Manifolds. arXiv:2509.02565
collisions.overlap: Capacity-allocation model; bounded manifold capacity. Implies features compete for capacity but does NOT propose sensitivity floor

candidates.differentiation_notes: The SENSITIVITY FLOOR mechanism (absorbed features lack specificity, non-absorbed features lack reliability, neither can achieve sensitivity) is genuinely novel. No prior work proposes complementary insufficiency through specificity/reliability tradeoff. The U-shaped relationship prediction and structural Q2/Q4 emptiness claim are novel testable predictions not found in prior literature.

candidates.title: Sensitivity-First: Sensitivity Failures Alone Explain Sanity Check

collisions.paper: Tian et al., 2025. Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity. arXiv:2509.23717
collisions.overlap: Documents that interpretable SAE features have POOR sensitivity; sensitivity declines with SAE width. Does NOT claim sensitivity failures ALONE explain Sanity Check or test whether absorption adds predictive value after controlling for sensitivity

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2026. Sanity Checks for Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2602.14111
collisions.overlap: Shows random baselines match SAEs on interpretability/causal editing. Does NOT identify which failure mode drives the crisis

candidates.differentiation_notes: The specific claim that sensitivity failures ALONE (without absorption) explain Sanity Check is not in Tian or any prior work. Tian documents sensitivity decline but does not claim it is the SOLE driver. The unique empirical contribution is the controlled test of whether absorption adds predictive value after controlling for sensitivity.

candidates.title: Geometric Density as Common Cause of Absorption and Sensitivity Failure

collisions.paper: Michaud et al., 2025. Understanding SAE Scaling with Feature Manifolds. arXiv:2509.02565
collisions.overlap: Capacity-allocation model; bounded manifold capacity; pathological regime where SAEs learn fewer features than latents. Does NOT propose geometric density as mediating variable between absorption and sensitivity

collisions.paper: Bereska et al., 2025. Superposition as Lossy Compression. arXiv:2512.13568
collisions.overlap: Information-theoretic framework connecting superposition to compression via Shannon entropy. Does NOT propose density as mediator

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2025. OrtSAE: Orthogonal Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2509.22033
collisions.overlap: Orthogonality penalty reduces absorption 65%; implies geometric structure matters. Does NOT propose density as common cause for BOTH absorption and sensitivity

candidates.differentiation_notes: The proposal of geometric density (measured via k-NN cosine similarity) as the SPECIFIC mediating variable between absorption and sensitivity is not in prior literature. Michaud discusses bounded capacity but not as a mediating variable. The specific mediation prediction (partial r < 0.3 after controlling for density) is a falsifiable claim not generated by existing work.

candidates.title: Layer-wise UAS Saturation: Measurement Path Forward
candidates.differentiation_notes: Systematic layer-wise UAS mapping is a descriptive contribution. Layer differences were not systematically mapped before. This directly addresses the Contrarian's layer-specificity objection and provides methodological foundation for Sensitivity Floor.

candidates.title: Steering Diffusion Explains Beta=20 Reversal

collisions.overlap: Contains the beta=20 reversal finding (low-absorption > high-absorption, p=0.015); mechanism was NOT identified in iter_004

collisions.paper: H6 falsification (internal): Decoder L2 norm ratio = 1.0
collisions.overlap: Prior hypothesis (decoder norm saturation) eliminated; steering diffusion provides alternative mechanism consistent with ratio=1.0

candidates.differentiation_notes: The steering diffusion mechanism (decoder-neighbor overlap causing dilution at high beta) is genuinely novel given H6 falsification. However, this is primarily a reanalysis of internal iter_004 data explaining an existing finding rather than a new discovery. Should be positioned as a control/alternative mechanism within the front-runner's experimental plan, not as a standalone candidate.

dropped_candidates_assessed.reason: H5 FALSIFIED (r=0.59, not independent), Q4=0, H6 FALSIFIED (ratio=1.0). The compound failure hypothesis assumed independence but pilot shows positive correlation.
dropped_candidates_assessed.collision_assessment: N/A - superseded by sensitivity floor which explains the positive correlation

dropped_candidates_assessed.reason: H1-R FALSIFIED (r=+0.36, not negative protective effect). Earlier r=-0.786 did not replicate across layers 4, 8, 10.
H6 FALSIFIED (L2 norm ratio=1.0). Decoder L2 norm saturation is not the mechanism for beta=20 reversal.
dropped_candidates_assessed.collision_assessment: Replaced by cand_steering_diffusion which proposes an alternative mechanism

changes_from_prior_iteration.score_adjustments: All scores re-evaluated based on deeper analysis of proposal framework

notes.assessment_basis: Proposal content (iter_009), candidate definitions (candidates.json), hypotheses (hypotheses.md), perspective documents (innovator.md, empiricist.md, theoretical.md, contrarian.md, pragmatist.md, interdisciplinary.md), alternatives.md, and internal pilot data references. External search tools (WebSearch, arXiv MCP, Google Scholar MCP) were unavailable during this check.
notes.confidence: HIGH for cand_sensitivity_floor (theoretical framework clearly distinct from cited prior work); MODERATE for others (conceptual connections exist but specific claims are not duplicated)
